clonic: adjective. Pertaining to clonus; having irregular, convulsive spasms.
clot: noun. A thrombus, solidified mass of blood.
coil: noun. Something wound in the form of a helix or spiral.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
colic: noun. Severe pains that grip the abdomen or the disease that causes such pains (due to intestinal or bowel-related problems).
colin: noun. The American quail or bobwhite, or related species.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
confit: noun. Any of various kinds of food that have been immersed in a substance for both flavor and preservation.
conflict: noun. A clash or disagreement, often violent, between two or more opposing groups or individuals.
fico: noun. a fig; an insignificant trifle
fil: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Filipino.
fin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Finnish.
fino: noun. The driest and palest type of traditional sherry.
fit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Meänkieli.
flic: noun. A data file containing computer animations.
flint: noun. A hard, fine-grained quartz that fractures conchoidally and generates sparks when struck against a material such as steel, because
flit: noun. A fluttering or darting movement.
floc: noun. A floccule; a soft or fluffy particle suspended in a liquid, or the fluffy mass of suspended particles so formed.
foil: noun. A very thin sheet of metal.
fon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Fon.
font: noun. A receptacle in a church for holy water, especially one used in baptism.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
lint: noun. Clinging fuzzy fluff that clings to fabric or accumulates in one's pockets or navel etc.
lion: noun. A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.
lit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lithuanian.
loft: noun. Air, the air; the sky, the heavens.
loin: noun. The part of the body (of humans and quadrupeds) at each side of the backbone, between the ribs and hips.
lot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lotuko.
lotic: adjective. Characterized by flowing water; swiftly flowing; concerned with flowing rivers, streams, etc.
nil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nila.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
noil: noun. A short fibre left over from combing wool or spinning silk during the preparation of textile yarns.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
otic: adjective. Of, relating/pertaining to, or located near the ear.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
til: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tillamook.
tin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tindi.
toil: noun. Labour, work, especially of a grueling nature.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
tonic: adjective. Pertaining to tension, especially of the muscles.
